reasoning: Confirmed. proxy_requests() fires on template_redirect with no authentication gate, nonce, or capability check — any visitor reaching a URL that matches a predefined HubSpot path pattern (/_hcms/, /hs/, /hubfs/, /hs-fs/, /cs/c/, /e3t/) or a cached proxy mapping triggers the proxy path. get_request_headers() collects the full Cookie header. filter_wordpress_cookies() (lines 431-461) uses a denylist of exactly three prefixes: 'wordpress_', 'wp-settings-', 'wp_woocommerce_'. The WooCommerce actual session cookie is named 'wc_session_cookie_<hash>' — its prefix 'wc_session_cookie_' does not match any blocked prefix and is forwarded unfiltered. Similarly PHPSESSID, security-plugin cookies (wordfence_verifiedHuman, sucuri_cloudproxy, etc.), and any other non-WP-prefixed cookie are forwarded. The target is the external HubSpot CDN at https://<portal_id>.sites-proxy.hscoscdn.net (confirmed via Filters::apply_sites_proxy_cdn_filters). No sanitizer or allowlist was missed — the code is purely denylist-based. Reachability is unauthenticated. Severity medium is correct: the recipient is HubSpot (a known third party, not an arbitrary attacker), but forwarding session cookies to a third-party CDN without user consent is a real data-leakage issue.
